title: cedrota guianensis
intro: Cedrota guianensis, also known as Cedronella or Wild Lime, is a tropical shrub native to the Guiana Shield region in South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cedrota guianensis include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in tea blends or applied topically as a poultice.
